articles.title: API levels rise in Sarawak, 17 areas register moderate air quality
articles.summary: KUCHING: Ambient air quality in Sarawak has shown a rising trend in the Air Pollutant Index (API), with 17 areas recording moderate levels and one area remaining in the good category. Read full story

articles.title: 17-year old killed in motorcycle crash
articles.summary: JERTIH: A 17-year-old boy was killed when the motorcycle he was riding hit another motorcycle in Kampung Telaga Nibong, Hulu Besut near here on Saturday (March 28). Read full story

articles.title: Foreign man nabbed for recording voyeuristic videos in mall toilet
articles.summary: PETALING JAYA: Police have arrested a foreign man for allegedly recording a video of another man inside a public toilet at a shopping mall in Bukit Jalil. Read full story

articles.title: Heatwave: 206 schools in Kota Setar, Pendang to implement PdPR until March 31
articles.summary: ALOR SETAR: Schools in Kota Setar and Pendang will shift to Home-Based Teaching and Learning (PdPR) for three days – from Sunday (March 29) until Tuesday (March 31) – due to the ongoing heatwave. Read

articles.title: Sultan Abdullah conducts aerial inspection of Pahang River
articles.summary: KUANTAN: The Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, has carried out an aerial inspection of the current water levels of Sungai Pahang, following the hot and dry weat

articles.title: Khairy keeps it low-key in bid to rejoin Umno
articles.summary: PONTIAN: Khairy Jamaluddin says he intends to return to Umno as an ordinary member without seeking any position as he is still in the process of rejoining the party. Read full story

articles.title: Two houses damaged in Taman Medan PJ fire
articles.summary: PETALING JAYA: Two houses have been damaged in a fire on Jalan PJS 2D/12, Taman Medan here. Read full story

articles.title: Ministry to meet restaurateurs over claims of 10-30% rise in operating costs
articles.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry will soon call in the Malaysian Muslim Restaurant Operators Association over claims that operating costs have risen by 10 to 30%. Read full st
